action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17 achieved a new best mean_r2=0.5318 using learned Ridge ensemble weights over 3 models. The bottleneck remains ptend_q (group R2=0.329). We have ~82 minutes and 13 iterations left. Analysis: Since ptend_q is the weakest group and contains 60 columns (47% of the mean_r2 average), improving it offers the highest ROI. Training a 4th model with higher loss weight on ptend_q columns should force the model to specialize on humidity tendencies. Ensembling this diverse model should lift the overall score without catastrophic forgetting risk (Ridge weights can downweight poor columns). Decision: Write train.py with configurable per-column loss weighting, write a robust compute_ensemble_weights.py, train a 2560x6 ResMLP from scratch with 3x weight on ptend_q (columns 60-119) for 15 epochs (~28 min), then update ensemble_config.json to 4 models, recompute stacking weights, and submit evaluation immediately.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 15 --batch_size 4096 --lr 1.5e-3 --dropout 0.1 --hidden_dim 2560 --num_blocks 6 --ckpt_name model_d.pt --loss_weight_q 3.0 --num_workers 8 && python -c "import json; cfg={'checkpoints':['checkpoints/best.pt','checkpoints/resmlp.pt','checkpoints/model_c.pt','checkpoints/model_d.pt']}; json.dump(cfg, open('ensemble_config.json','w')); print('Updated ensemble_config.json')" && python compute_ensemble_weights.py
